_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to use our growing knowledge of letter patterns, spelling rules, word families and word origins to spell with accuracy and confidence.

_source.w: We are learning to use our growing knowledge of letter patterns, spelling rules, word families and word origins to spell with accuracy and confidence.
y: Strong spelling skills give our writing polish and credibility.
Using multiple strategies — patterns, rules, morphology and etymology — gives us the best chance of spelling correctly.
Confident spelling means we can focus on expressing our ideas rather than worrying about getting words right.
eg: We use knowledge of word origins to spell words with unusual patterns — like Greek-origin words with 'ph'.
We apply spelling generalisations and know the common exceptions.
We use morphological word families to check and correct our spelling.
We identify the base word in a complex word and use it as a spelling anchor.
We use multiple strategies when unsure — trying the word visually, checking with morphology, consulting a reference.
We maintain a personal spelling journal with words we find challenging and the strategies that help.
